content.text: How do I ensure a directory structure exists on the filesystem without causing an error if some subdirectory is already present?
What happens if I try to use this function on a path that currently holds an existing file instead of a directory?
The function will not gracefully handle that case; it will raise an exception because os.makedirs() is expecting a directory or doesn't support creating over a file.
That makes sense—it really relies on the input being a safe location to create.
